182.reason: [This method returns the token before current index, but when an expected token (like SEMICN) is missing, the current token is not advanced yet, so lookBehind() points to a token that may be unrelated to the missing symbol. This leads to incorrect line number reporting for errors like 'i', 'j', 'k'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This method returns the token before current index, but when an expected token (like SEMICN) is missing, the current token is not advanced yet, so lookBehind() points to a token that may be unrelated to the missing symbol. This leads to incorrect line number reporting for errors like 'i', 'j', 'k'.]

258.reason: [The error line number calculation is incorrect. According to the problem description, for missing semicolon error 'i', the line number should correspond to the line where the last non-terminal before the semicolon is located. However, lookBehind().getLine() + 1 may not correctly identify this line because lookBehind() returns the previous token which might not be the last meaningful token of the declaration. Additionally, the parser continues execution after adding the error instead of handling the missing semicolon properly, which can cause cascading errors.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Using lookBehind().getLine() + 1 is inconsistent with specification: error 'i' should report the line of the last non-terminal before the missing semicolon, but lookBehind() may point to an unrelated token. Also, adding 1 assumes 0-based line numbers, but lines are already 1-based from lexer.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [The error line number calculation is incorrect. According to the problem description, for missing semicolon error 'i', the line number should correspond to the line where the last non-terminal before the semicolon is located. However, lookBehind().getLine() + 1 may not correctly identify this line because lookBehind() returns the previous token which might not be the last meaningful token of the declaration. Additionally, the parser continues execution after adding the error instead of handling the missing semicolon properly, which can cause cascading errors.]

297.reason: [Using lookBehind().getLine() gives wrong line number because when RBRACK is missing, the parser hasn't consumed any token after the expression inside brackets, so lookBehind() points to the last token of ConstExp, which might be on a different line than where the bracket was opened. According to spec, error 'k' should report the line of the last preceding non-terminal before the missing ']'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Using lookBehind().getLine() gives wrong line number because when RBRACK is missing, the parser hasn't consumed any token after the expression inside brackets, so lookBehind() points to the last token of ConstExp, which might be on a different line than where the bracket was opened. According to spec, error 'k' should report the line of the last preceding non-terminal before the missing ']'.]
